MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS CARLOS BOYER, JOHNNY BOYER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Plaintiff failed to maintain his burden of production per CCP 437c(p)(1).

"A plaintiff has met his or her burden of showing that there is no defense to a cause of action if that party has proved each element of the cause of action entitling the party to judgment on the cause of action." (CCP 437c(p)(1).) "A motion for summary adjudication shall be granted only if it completely disposes of a cause of action" (CCP 437c(f)(1).)

In plaintiff's complaint, he prays for damages on causes of action one through fifteen and restitution/disgorgement on cause of action sixteen. Plaintiff's separate statement of facts provides no evidence regarding what amount of money is allegedly owed to plaintiff. Plaintiff therefore has not maintained his burden of production because there is no evidence regarding the damages element. (See Paramount Petroleum Corp. v. Superior Court (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226 [court lacked power to grant summary adjudication on issue of liability and leave issue of damages for trial].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT A1 PROTECTIVE SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Quash And For Protective Order Re: Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To City And County Of San Francisco; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The motion to quash is DENIED. The subpoena, as modified by agreement between Plaintiff's counsel and the San Francisco City Attorney's office seeks discoverable documents. In particular, the records sought by the subpoena are relevant, at minimum, to the legal claims set forth in the 8th , 9th, 12th, and 14th causes of action. The Defendant's argument that there is no private right of action under the HCSO is unpersuasive as the Plaintiff has not pled any such cause of action, nor has he argued that requested documents would be relevant to establishing any such claim. Defendant's objections to the Plaintiff's evidence in support of his motion are overruled. Both parties' request for sanctions are DENIED.

Order signed after hearing.
